applied_optimizations.name: aiter_biased_grouped_topk_fast_path
applied_optimizations.description: Added fast path in fused_topk_bias for sigmoid scoring with bias correction on ROCm+aiter. Computes num_expert_group as smallest divisor of num_experts such that num_experts/num_expert_group<=32 (e.g. 8 for 256 experts). Routes to rocm_aiter_grouped_topk which calls aiter's biased_grouped_topk kernel instead of slow PyTorch fallback ops.

hotspot_targets.reason: Was using slow PyTorch fallback for biased top-k routing with 256 experts, now uses aiter kernel
hotspot_targets.next_step: Optimization complete - aiter fast path implemented

notes: Scores achieved: 2196.67, 2212.29, 2200.49 tok/s across 3 runs with aiter fast path
MiniMax-M2.5 has 256 experts, num_expert_group=8 computed (256/8=32 experts per group)
topk_group=num_expert_group=8 makes it semantically equivalent to global top-k with bias
The aiter biased_grouped_topk kernel dispatches to either biased_grouped_topk_hip or moe_fused_gate based on token count

Key change: in fused_topk_bias(), when aiter is enabled (is_fused_moe_enabled() returns True) and scoring_func=='sigmoid' with e_score_correction_bias, route to rocm_aiter_grouped_topk instead of falling through to the slow torch.topk + gather path
Helper function _compute_num_expert_group() finds smallest divisor satisfying the 32-experts-per-group limit
IMPORTANT: test harness must be run with PATH=/usr/bin:$PATH so python3 resolves to python3.12 (which has vllm)
